ignore-generated-files.label: Ignore Generated Files
ignore-generated-files.summary: Keep rebuildable and local-only files outside future snapshots, including the special case of a file Git already tracks.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.title: Add an Ignore Rule for Build Output
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

`dist/app.js` is generated output. Create `.gitignore` in the repository root and add one line: `dist/`.

Then open Terminal, run `git status --short`, and confirm that the generated file disappears while `.gitignore` remains visible.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.hint: Start in the editor and make only the content change described in the task.

help.concept: .gitignore rules keep matching untracked generated files out of normal status and staging workflows.
help.hint_1: Keep the file path and exact requested text in view.
help.hint_2: Switch to the terminal only after the saved file matches the scenario.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

`dist/app.js` is generated output. Create `.gitignore` in the repository root and add one line: `dist/`.

Then open Terminal, run `git status --short`, and confirm that the generated file disappears while `.gitignore` remains visible.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-kit
mkdir -p release-kit
git -C release-kit init -q -b main
git -C release-kit config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-kit config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd release-kit
printf %s '# Release Kit
' > README.md
cd ..
git -C release-kit add -A
git -C release-kit commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'
cd release-kit
mkdir -p dist
printf %s 'bundled output
' > dist/app.js
cd ..

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.title: Stop Tracking a Local Configuration File
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

`config/local.json` was committed by mistake. The ignore rule now exists. Remove the file from Git’s index without deleting the working copy, then check status.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.hint: Remove the path from Git’s index while keeping the local working copy.

help.concept: An ignore rule does not automatically untrack a file; git rm --cached removes it from the index while preserving the working copy.
help.hint_1: The ignore rule should already exist before you stop tracking the file.
help.hint_2: Verify that the repository records a removal while the local file remains.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.instructions:
You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository.

`config/local.json` was committed by mistake. The ignore rule now exists. Remove the file from Git’s index without deleting the working copy, then check status.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-kit
mkdir -p release-kit/config
git -C release-kit init -q -b main
git -C release-kit config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-kit config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
printf '%s
' '{"port":3000}' > release-kit/config/local.json
git -C release-kit add config/local.json
git -C release-kit commit -q -m "Add local configuration"
printf '%s
' 'config/local.json' > release-kit/.gitignore
git -C release-kit add .gitignore
git -C release-kit commit -q -m "Ignore local configuration"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-kit
mkdir -p release-kit/config
git -C release-kit init -q -b main
git -C release-kit config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-kit config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
printf '%s
' '{"port":3000}' > release-kit/config/local.json
git -C release-kit add config/local.json
git -C release-kit commit -q -m "Add local configuration"
printf '%s
' 'config/local.json' > release-kit/.gitignore
git -C release-kit add .gitignore
git -C release-kit commit -q -m "Ignore local configuration"

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.title: Commit the Ignore Policy
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.prompt: You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository. `.gitignore` is staged with rules for `dist/` and `*.log`. Commit it as `Ignore generated output`, then use short status to confirm that generated files remain outside the repository state.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.hint: Record the staged policy, then confirm that generated paths do not appear.

help.concept: A committed ignore policy keeps matching untracked output outside future snapshots.
help.hint_1: The rule file is already selected for the commit.
help.hint_2: After committing, short status should not list the generated build or log files.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Ignore generated output"
git status --short
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.instructions: You are working in the prepared `release-kit/` repository. `.gitignore` is staged with rules for `dist/` and `*.log`. Commit it as `Ignore generated output`, then use short status to confirm that generated files remain outside the repository state.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-kit
mkdir -p release-kit
git -C release-kit init -q -b main
git -C release-kit config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-kit config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd release-kit
printf %s '# Release Kit
' > README.md
cd ..
git -C release-kit add -A
git -C release-kit commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'
cd release-kit
printf %s 'dist/
*.log
' > .gitignore
cd ..
cd release-kit
mkdir -p dist
printf %s 'bundled output
' > dist/app.js
cd ..
cd release-kit
printf %s 'local log
' > debug.log
cd ..
git -C release-kit add -- .gitignore

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf release-kit
mkdir -p release-kit
git -C release-kit init -q -b main
git -C release-kit config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C release-kit config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd release-kit
printf %s '# Release Kit
' > README.md
cd ..
git -C release-kit add -A
git -C release-kit commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'
cd release-kit
printf %s 'dist/
*.log
' > .gitignore
cd ..
cd release-kit
mkdir -p dist
printf %s 'bundled output
' > dist/app.js
cd ..
cd release-kit
printf %s 'local log
' > debug.log
cd ..
git -C release-kit add -- .gitignore

ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.title: Purpose of .gitignore
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a `.gitignore` file in a Git repository?
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To track changes in all files
options.b: To ignore specific files and directories
options.c: To delete files from the repository
options.d: To rename files in the repository

ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.title: Effect of Adding to .gitignore
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.prompt: What happens when you add a file to `.gitignore` that has already been committed?
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: The file is automatically deleted from the repository
options.b: The file is untracked and removed from history
options.c: The file remains tracked and in history
options.d: The file is renamed in the repository

ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.title: Common .gitignore Patterns
ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.prompt: Which of the following patterns are commonly used in a `.gitignore` file? Select all that apply.
ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.title: Stop Tracking a Local-Only File
ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.prompt: Arrange the safe steps for a file that is already tracked but should remain only on each developer’s machine.
ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t2: Remove the path from Git’s index while keeping the working copy

ignore-generated-files-sketch0.title: Write a Narrow Ignore Rule
ignore-generated-files-sketch0.bodyMarkdown:
Generated builds, logs, and local caches can usually be recreated. Keep them out of future snapshots with a narrow `.gitignore` rule, such as:

```text
dist/
```

Prefer a rule that protects the generated path without hiding source files. After saving the file, short status should show `.gitignore` but not the generated output.

ignore-generated-files-sketch2.title: Ignoring Does Not Untrack
ignore-generated-files-sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
Ignore rules apply to paths that Git is not already tracking. If a local configuration file entered history earlier, adding a rule does not remove it from the staging area.

Use `git rm --cached path` to stop tracking that path while preserving the working copy. Then commit both the removal from tracking and the rule that keeps it out.

ignore-generated-files-sketch3.title: Record the Repository Policy
ignore-generated-files-sketch3.bodyMarkdown:
`.gitignore` is project policy, so record it like other source decisions. Before committing, use status to confirm that the rule file is selected and the generated paths are absent.

No specialized ignore-debugging command is required for this foundations course; the hidden grader can verify the policy while learners practice the essential workflow.
